name: Higgs-portal scalar dark matter
summary: The minimal renormalisable extension of the SM that produces a stable dark matter candidate: a real (or complex) singlet scalar S protected by Z_2, coupling to the SM only through |H|^2 |S|^2. Three new parameters set DM mass, self-coupling, and Higgs-portal coupling lambda; thermal freeze-out determines lambda once the relic abundance is fixed.

addresses_puzzles.note: Direct-detection bounds (LZ 2024) close most of the m_S < m_H/2 'invisible Higgs decay' window; viable parameter space remains around the m_S ~ m_H/2 resonance and m_S > ~few hundred GeV.

touches_benchmarks.note: Spin-independent S-nucleon cross section sigma_SI ~ lambda^2 m_p^4 / (m_H^4 m_S^2) is competitive with LZ 9.2e-48 cm^2 bound at m_S ~ tens of GeV; resonance and high-mass regions remain.
If 2 m_S < m_H, invisible Higgs decay constraints (BR_inv < ~10%) bound lambda; current LHC limits already exclude most of this regime.

new_fields: real or complex scalar singlet S
introduces.note: Mass m_S, self-coupling, and Higgs-portal coupling lambda; relic abundance fixes one combination.

history: Silveira and Zee (1985), 'Scalar phantoms', Phys. Lett. B 161, 136. McDonald (1994, hep-ph/9305312) systematic analysis. Burgess, Pospelov, ter Veldhuis (2001), 'The minimal model of nonbaryonic dark matter: a singlet scalar', Nucl. Phys. B 619, 709. Active LHC + direct detection target through 2010s-2020s.

references.citation: Silveira and Zee, 'Scalar phantoms', Phys. Lett. B 161, 136 (1985)
Burgess, Pospelov, ter Veldhuis, 'The minimal model of nonbaryonic dark matter: a singlet scalar', Nucl. Phys. B 619, 709 (2001)
